attributes.stem:
A 60-year-old man presents to the clinic with a three month history of abdominal pain, bloating, and unintentional weight loss of 10 kg. He has also noticed a decrease in his appetite, early satiety and occasional vomiting. He has a medical history of hypertension and chronic gastritis for which he has been taking lisinopril and proton pump inhibitors for several years. He has a 15 pack-year smoking history and occasionally drinks wine.

On examination his blood pressure is 135/89 mmHg, pulse 91/min and temperature 37.0o. The rest of the physical examination is unremarkable. A gastrointestinal endoscopy finds a large ulcerating mass in the gastric body and a biopsy confirms gastric adenocarcinoma. A CT and PET scan of the abdomen and pelvis show a locally advanced tumour with involvement of coeliac lymph nodes. A diagnosis of stage IIIb gastric adenocarcinoma is made. He is considered to be a candidate for surgical resection and will receive peri-operative chemotherapy. There are several different regimens to consider.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following is the most important factor in determining the appropriate chemotherapy regimen?
options: Expression of PD-L1 on cancer cell
attributes.explanation:
Based on the given information, the diagnosis for this patient would be stage IIIb gastric adenocarcinoma with nodal metastases. In patients who have resectable gastric cancer, the most commonly used regimen involves peri-operative chemotherapy with surgical resection of the tumour and lymph nodes. There are two main chemotherapy regimens, namely the FLOT (Fluorouracil, Leucovorin, Oxaliplatin and Docetaxel) and CAPOX (XELOX) (capecitabine and oxaliplatin). The main determining factor for whether the FLOT or CAPOX regimen should be pursued depends on the patient's performance status, of which the most commonly used score is ECOG. ECOG measures a patient’s level of functioning and is graded on a scale of 0-5.

  • ECOG 0: Normal

  • ECOG 1: Restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out work of a light or sedentary nature

  • ECOG 2: Ambulatory and capable of all self-care but unable to work

  • ECOG 3: Limited self-care; spends > 1/2 day confined to bed or chair

  • ECOG 4: Completely disabled; bed-bound; unable to self-care

  • ECOG 5: Dead

eGFR, platelets and absolute neutrophil count are important markers that need to be monitored during or before chemotherapy, as they would impact the dosage and timing of administration. However, they do not impact the choice of chemotherapy.

Emerging evidence shows promising results when immune checkpoint inhibitor therapies were used against gastric adenocarcinoma. Nivolumab, a PD-1 monoclonal antibody is now recommended in patients with complete surgically resected stage II or III oesophageal or gastro-oesophageal junction cancer with residual pathological disease who have received prior neo-adjuvant chemoradiation therapy. The commencement of nivolumab however does not rely on the expression of PD-L1 on cancer cells, but rather on the patient's ECOG score.
learningPoints: ECOG is the most important factor in determining chemotherapy regimen
ANC, eGFR and platelets should be monitored prior and during chemotherapy to modify the dosage of chemotherapy
references: Marmon, H., & Enzinger, P. (2022, October 21). Adjuvant and neoadjuvant treatment of gastric cancer. UpToDate. https://www.uptodate.com/contents/adjuvant-and-neoadjuvant-treatment-of-gastric-cancer?search=gastric%20adenocarcinoma&source=search_result&selectedTitle=2%7E150&usage_type=default&display_rank=2#H1169910879

eviQ. (2022, December 8). Gastroesophageal and oesophageal adjuvant nivolumab. https://www.eviq.org.au/medical-oncology/upper-gastrointestinal/gastric-and-oesophageal-adjuvant-and-neoadjuvant/4108-gastroesophageal-and-oesophageal-adjuvant-niv#side-effects

eviQ. (2021, August 6). Adjuvant capox (XELOX) (Capecitabine and Oxaliplatin). https://www.eviq.org.au/medical-oncology/colorectal/adjuvant-and-neoadjuvant/4019-adjuvant-capox-xelox-capecitabine-and-oxal

eviQ. (2018, March 6). Gastric and gastroesophageal neoadjuvant/adjuvant flot (fluorouracil leucovorin oxaliplatin and docetaxel). https://www.eviq.org.au/medical-oncology/upper-gastrointestinal/gastric-and-oesophageal-adjuvant-and-neoadjuvant/2038-gastric-and-gastroesophageal-neoadjuvant-adju
